column1: Tab 5 of Username_OSINT_DataPoints.xlsx — Cross-platform identity correlation & confidence scoring engine

column6: CONFIDENCE IMPACT
column7: TECHNICAL NOTES & IMPLEMENTATION

column3: Instagram / Twitter / Telegram / Facebook / ShareChat / LinkedIn
column6: VERY HIGH — single strongest indicator; same handle across platforms = near-certain same person
column7: Case-insensitive comparison. Normalise @prefix. If exact match on ≥2 platforms → auto-flag confidence ≥90%. Store as primary key in correlation DB.

column6: VERY HIGH — phone is a real-world anchor; rarely coincidental across platforms
column7: Regex: [6-9]\d{9} (India). Normalise +91/0 prefix. Run through Truecaller API for name resolution. Cross-check with police records.

column6: VERY HIGH — email is a direct identifier linking accounts & real identity
column7: Normalise case. Flag burner domains (mailnull, guerrilla). Feed into HIBP/breach DB lookup for associated accounts.

column6: VERY HIGH — reused photo is strong proof; works even after crop/filter/resize
column7: Compute pHash (8×8 DCT). Hamming distance ≤10 = match. Supplement with face embedding (DeepFace) for altered photos. Flag if same pic used on 3+ platforms.

column6: HIGH — slight variations (lav_ig, lavkumar_tw) are deliberate alias patterns
column7: Levenshtein distance ≤2 OR shared root token. Strip platform suffixes (_ig, _tw, _real). Score by token overlap. Confidence +10 if combined with bio match.

column6: HIGH — same face even after heavy filter or reframe is strong identity confirmation
column7: DeepFace / FaceNet embedding; cosine similarity ≥0.75. Use only when pHash fails. Requires GPU. Flag for manual review if 0.65–0.74.

column6: HIGH — recurring tags/mentions across platforms reveal real-world associations
column7: Build adjacency list of @mentions and tags. Identify nodes appearing on ≥2 platforms. Recurring tags from same person = strong link. Visualise as network graph.

column6: MEDIUM-HIGH — copied bio is deliberate; partial match still significant
column7: Cosine similarity on TF-IDF vectors. Threshold ≥0.80 = strong match; 0.60–0.79 = flag for review. Normalise Hindi/regional text (Unicode NFC). Strip emojis before compare.

column6: LOW-MEDIUM — "Mumbai" alone is weak; specific locality or neighbourhood is stronger
column7: Fuzzy match (Jaro-Winkler ≥0.88). Geocode both strings; if lat/lng within 10km = match. Weight increases to 18 if combined with username or pic match.

column6: MEDIUM — shared followers suggest real-world overlap; strong when >20% overlap
column7: Jaccard index on follower sets. Requires login-based access; rate-limited. Threshold ≥0.20 = flag. Most useful for confirmation, not discovery.

column6: MEDIUM — posting at same hours on multiple platforms suggests same operator timezone
column7: Extract post timestamps → convert to IST → bin by hour. Compare hourly histograms across platforms. Pearson correlation ≥0.70 = flag. Strong indicator for bot or coordinated accounts.

column6: MEDIUM — niche hashtag sets are fingerprints; broad hashtags (#love) are noise
column7: Extract top-20 hashtags per account. Jaccard on tag sets. Filter stopword hashtags. Niche overlap (e.g. #delhigraffiti + #streetsofdelhi) is a stronger signal than generic ones.

column6: HIGH — personal website or unique URL is a strong unique identifier
column7: Exact domain match = weight 18. Path match = weight 22. Resolve short URLs (bit.ly, linktr.ee). Scrape destination for contact info / other social links.

column6: LOW-MEDIUM — useful as a tiebreaker or to rule out; alone is weak evidence
column7: Compute type-token ratio, avg sentence length, punctuation patterns, Hinglish mixing ratio. Compare with cosine similarity on stylometric vector. Use only to confirm, never as primary signal.

column2: Same Device / Browser Fingerprint (if leaked in metadata)
column6: MEDIUM — device model in old EXIF or API responses can link accounts from same device
column7: Instagram strips EXIF post-2012 but some 3rd-party posts may leak. Extract via PIL/Exiftool. Device model + OS version = partial fingerprint. Combine with timing data.

column2: Shared Contact Info via Linktree / Bio Link
column6: HIGH — Linktree or similar aggregator pages often expose multiple platform links at once
column7: Fetch bio URL → parse all outbound links → identify social platform URLs → extract usernames. Single request can reveal 4–6 platform handles simultaneously. High ROI.

column6: VERY HIGH — each platform bio link = directed edge in identity graph; traversal reveals full persona cluster
column7: Build directed graph: node=username, edge=bio_link_to. BFS from seed node. Cluster connected components = likely same person. Visualise with NetworkX / Gephi.

column6: HIGH — Telegram syncs contacts; adding target's number reveals their Telegram handle if contact sync is on
column7: Legal method: Use a registered test SIM. Add target number to contacts → open Telegram → check if profile appears. Reveals handle + display name. Requires legal authorisation in India (IT Act).

column2: Reverse Image Search on Profile Picture
column6: HIGH — same pic used on forums, dating apps, or older accounts links to wider identity trail
column7: Submit to Google Images, TinEye, Yandex (best for faces), Bing Visual Search. Yandex is most effective for Indian faces. Automate with search API. Archive all results.

column6: VERY HIGH — breach data reveals associated usernames, passwords (for pattern analysis), and other linked accounts
column7: Query HaveIBeenPwned API. Use Dehashed / IntelX for deeper lookup (legal in LE context). Reveals: linked usernames, old passwords (for pattern analysis), breach source platforms.

column6: LOW-MEDIUM — accounts created within days of each other on multiple platforms suggest coordinated setup
column7: Estimate Instagram creation from first post + account ID range. Twitter shows join date. If ±30 days = flag. Strong indicator when combined with exact username match.

column6: MEDIUM — if subject's known associate follows Account B, Account B may be the same subject on another platform
column7: Seed from known associate list. Check follow relationships. Useful when direct signals are absent. Requires follower list access (login). Build second-degree association map.

column6: MEDIUM — same pinned post, poem, or slogan deliberately chosen = identity marker
column7: Extract pinned post text/image. Compare across platforms with text similarity + pHash. Unique quotes, poems, or manifestos are stronger signals than generic motivational quotes.

column6: HIGH — name + employer + profile pic on LinkedIn matches social handle = near-confirmed identity
VERY HIGH — GitHub commit emails or profile email matching another platform is a near-certain identity link
VERY HIGH — GitHub profile fields allow direct Twitter/website links, self-disclosed by the user
HIGH — personal details voluntarily shared in Reddit comments (location, job, age) corroborate other platform bios
VERY HIGH — Reddit allows direct social account linking on profile, self-disclosed by user
MEDIUM — long-form comments on GitHub issues/Reddit posts provide rich stylometric data for cross-matching
FINAL OUTPUT — sum of matched signal weights → map to confidence tier
column7:
Score 0–29: LOW (flag for review)
Score 30–54: MEDIUM (probable same person, manual verify)
Score 55–79: HIGH (likely same person, document evidence)
Score 80+: VERY HIGH (near-certain, actionable intelligence)
